threats.title: VPC without flow logs enabled
threats.description: The VPC does not have flow logs configured. Without flow logs, network traffic patterns cannot be analyzed for security incidents or anomalies.
threats.mitigation: Enable VPC flow logs and direct them to CloudWatch Logs or S3 for analysis. Consider using a REJECT-only filter to reduce log volume while capturing blocked traffic.

threats.title: S3 bucket without server-side encryption
threats.description: The S3 bucket does not have server-side encryption configured. Data at rest may be accessible if storage media is compromised.
threats.mitigation: Enable server-side encryption using AES-256 (SSE-S3) or AWS KMS (SSE-KMS) via aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration.

threats.title: S3 bucket without versioning
threats.description: The S3 bucket does not have versioning enabled. Without versioning, deleted or overwritten objects cannot be recovered, and tampering is harder to detect.
threats.mitigation: Enable bucket versioning via aws_s3_bucket_versioning resource to protect against accidental deletion and enable audit trails.

threats.title: S3 bucket without access logging
threats.description: The S3 bucket does not have access logging configured. Without logging, unauthorized access attempts cannot be detected and actions cannot be attributed to specific actors.
threats.mitigation: Enable server access logging via aws_s3_bucket_logging resource, directing logs to a separate dedicated logging bucket.

threats.title: S3 bucket allows public access
threats.description: The S3 bucket ACL or policy allows public read or write access. This can lead to data exposure or unauthorized data modification.
threats.mitigation: Set ACL to 'private' and use aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block to block all public access at the bucket level.

threats.title: S3 bucket without versioning
threats.description: The S3 bucket does not have versioning enabled. Without versioning, deleted or overwritten objects cannot be recovered, and tampering is harder to detect.
threats.mitigation: Enable bucket versioning via aws_s3_bucket_versioning resource to protect against accidental deletion and enable audit trails.

threats.title: S3 bucket without access logging
threats.description: The S3 bucket does not have access logging configured. Without logging, unauthorized access attempts cannot be detected and actions cannot be attributed to specific actors.
threats.mitigation: Enable server access logging via aws_s3_bucket_logging resource, directing logs to a separate dedicated logging bucket.

threats.title: IAM policy with wildcard actions
threats.description: The IAM policy grants wildcard (*) actions, providing unrestricted access to AWS services. This violates least privilege and could allow an attacker to escalate privileges across the account.
threats.mitigation: Replace wildcard actions with specific, scoped actions following the principle of least privilege. Use AWS Access Analyzer to identify required permissions.

threats.title: EC2 instance with public IP address
threats.description: The EC2 instance is configured with a public IP address, directly exposing it to internet traffic. This increases the attack surface significantly.
threats.mitigation: Remove public IP association and use a load balancer or bastion host for access. Place instances in private subnets.

threats.title: EC2 instance without detailed monitoring
threats.description: The EC2 instance does not have detailed monitoring enabled. Without monitoring, unauthorized access or resource abuse may go undetected and actions cannot be attributed.
threats.mitigation: Enable detailed monitoring and configure CloudWatch alarms for suspicious activity patterns.

threats.title: EC2 instance with unencrypted root volume
threats.description: The EC2 instance root block device is not encrypted. Data on the volume can be accessed if the underlying storage media is compromised.
threats.mitigation: Enable EBS encryption on all volumes using AWS-managed or customer-managed KMS keys.

threats.title: Lambda function not attached to VPC
threats.description: The Lambda function runs outside a VPC, meaning it has direct internet access and cannot leverage VPC security controls like security groups and NACLs.
threats.mitigation: Attach the Lambda function to a VPC with appropriate security groups. Use VPC endpoints for AWS service access.

threats.title: Lambda function without dead letter queue
threats.description: The Lambda function does not have a dead letter queue configured. Failed invocations may be silently lost, affecting reliability and making it difficult to diagnose processing failures.
threats.mitigation: Configure a dead letter queue (SQS or SNS) to capture failed invocations for retry or investigation.

threats.title: S3 bucket without server-side encryption
threats.description: The S3 bucket does not have server-side encryption configured. Data at rest may be accessible if storage media is compromised.
threats.mitigation: Enable server-side encryption using AES-256 (SSE-S3) or AWS KMS (SSE-KMS) via aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration.

threats.title: S3 bucket without versioning
threats.description: The S3 bucket does not have versioning enabled. Without versioning, deleted or overwritten objects cannot be recovered, and tampering is harder to detect.
threats.mitigation: Enable bucket versioning via aws_s3_bucket_versioning resource to protect against accidental deletion and enable audit trails.

threats.title: S3 bucket without access logging
threats.description: The S3 bucket does not have access logging configured. Without logging, unauthorized access attempts cannot be detected and actions cannot be attributed to specific actors.
threats.mitigation: Enable server access logging via aws_s3_bucket_logging resource, directing logs to a separate dedicated logging bucket.

threats.title: Trust boundary crossing: dmz -> private
threats.description: Data flows from aws_instance.gpu_experiment_box (dmz zone) to aws_subnet.ml_private_a (private zone), crossing a trust boundary. This flow should be authenticated, encrypted, and validated.
threats.mitigation: Ensure all data crossing trust boundaries is encrypted in transit (TLS/mTLS), authenticated, and validated at the receiving end.

threats.title: Trust boundary crossing: dmz -> private
threats.description: Data flows from aws_instance.gpu_experiment_box (dmz zone) to aws_security_group.notebook_debug (private zone), crossing a trust boundary. This flow should be authenticated, encrypted, and validated.
threats.mitigation: Ensure all data crossing trust boundaries is encrypted in transit (TLS/mTLS), authenticated, and validated at the receiving end.
